title: House GOP’s ‘big beautiful bill’ cancels millions for Syracuse to revitalize poor neighborhood near I-81
summary: House Republicans' 'One Big Beautiful Bill Act' cancels $30 million in unspent Federal Highway Administration grants awarded to Syracuse for I-81 neighborhood revitalization. Syracuse had received only $300,000 of its $30 million grant and was awaiting environmental review completion before accessing remaining funds. The bill permanently rescinds all unspent Neighborhood Access and Equity Program funds ($2+ billion nationally) to reduce federal deficit. Senate Democrats, including Sen. Schumer, plan to restore funding.
department_refs: Office of the Chief Innovation Officer

political_signal.description: Republican-controlled House has eliminated competitive federal grant funding for Syracuse I-81 revitalization project. Democratic Senate leadership (Schumer) signals intent to restore funding. Grant program cancellation reflects partisan budget prioritization (deficit reduction vs. community development).

processing_notes: Article focuses on federal legislative impact, not direct CIO action. CIO stakeholder is Greg Loh (Chief Policy Officer), who is coordinating restoration efforts.

Dollar amount extraction: $30M (Syracuse award), $3.3B (program total), $2B+ (rescinded nationally), $180M (combined NYS/Syracuse award), $150M (NYS DOT secured), $300K (Syracuse received to date), $1B (red/purple state risk).
No Syracuse city departments directly referenced; state DOT and federal agencies are primary actors.
I-81 project is major infrastructure/equity initiative for CIO visibility. Environmental review timeline (2 years) critical to funding access.
Advocacy channel: Senate negotiations provide restoration opportunity.
